open2.title: How to Answer Well
open2.body: Name a genuine skill gap and describe active steps to close it. Avoid generic answers like 'get better at design.' Instead: 'I want to improve my motion design skills. I'm taking a 12-week animation course, practicing 30 minutes daily, and sharing progress in an online community for feedback.' Show specific actions and accountability. Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tJxFZrMxT3s

examplesByCategory.comp_power: Creative Computational Power questions assess your ability to use data and analytics to inform creative decisions. Strong performers validate assumptions with evidence, test creative options systematically, and balance intuition with measurable insights.
examplesByCategory.communication: Creative Communication questions evaluate how you present ideas, receive feedback, and align stakeholders. Effective creative communicators translate abstract concepts into tangible examples and adapt their approach for different audiences.
examplesByCategory.knowledge: Creative Knowledge questions measure how you stay current in your craft and build domain expertise. Top creative performers combine formal learning with community engagement and apply new techniques quickly to their work.
examplesByCategory.creativity: Creative Creativity questions assess your idea generation process and ability to produce original work. Strong performers embrace constraints, seek diverse inspiration, and prototype quickly to validate creative directions.
examplesByCategory.drive: Creative Drive questions measure your resilience, motivation, and ability to maintain creative momentum. High-drive creatives celebrate small wins, learn from rejection, and protect dedicated time for deep creative work.

service_ops.title: Create Your XIMAtar
service_ops.subtitle: Complete our comprehensive assessment to discover your professional strengths

q1.question: When service metrics dip, your first step is to:
options: Segment by shift/location
Check process compliance
Compare with historical patterns
Verify data integrity

q2.question: A process change you've implemented is causing confusion among frontline staff. Some are reverting to old procedures. You can't mandate compliance without understanding the breakdown. How do you diagnose and address the communication gap?
options: Reissue the procedure documentation with step-by-step detail, numbered exceptions, and explicit comparisons to the old process
Explain the why behind the change — connect the new process to outcomes staff care about, rather than reissuing the how
Spend a shift observing where staff revert, ask them what's unclear in their own words, then revise communication based on actual confusion points
Replace written instructions with a short walkthrough video showing the new process in context, supplemented by a quick-reference card

q3.question: Your team follows a standard procedure, but an unusual case doesn't fit the existing protocol. The procedure manual doesn't cover this scenario. You:
options: Identify which parts of the standard procedure still apply and adapt the rest with documented reasoning
Consult a colleague who's handled similar edge cases to understand how they approached it
Escalate to a supervisor with a clear description of why the standard procedure doesn't fit
Handle it using your best judgment and write up the case so the procedure can be updated

q4.question: Your service team needs to reduce average handling time by 20%, but cutting steps would hurt quality scores. You can't hire more staff. You:
options: Redesign the service flow so that common cases follow a streamlined path while complex cases get the full process — handling both faster on average
Identify the most time-consuming step and find a way to perform it in parallel with another step rather than sequentially
Redefine 'handling time' by shifting some steps to asynchronous follow-up, reducing live interaction time without cutting content
Create templated responses for the most frequent 30% of requests, freeing agents to spend more time on the remaining complex cases

q5.question: Your team's incident response time has been slower than the target, and you are part of the problem. You:
options: Shadow the fastest responder on shift and document what they do differently
Review your last five incidents to find the specific step where you lost time
Practice responding to simulated incidents with a timer to build speed
Redesign your personal triage checklist based on what has actually slowed you down

q6.question: To schedule staff effectively, you:
options: Model demand by hour
Account for skill mix
Plan buffers for spikes
Review constraints and rules

q7.question: A customer is escalating a complaint. They're factually wrong about what happened, but emotionally they've had a genuinely poor experience. Your response will be reviewed by your manager. How do you handle the factual inaccuracy while addressing the experience?
options: Acknowledge their frustration, then walk through what actually occurred with timestamps and specifics — clarity prevents future disputes
Focus the response on the experience gap rather than the factual sequence — 'regardless of specifics, the outcome fell short' — and propose resolution
Ask the customer to confirm specific details of their experience — let them self-correct factual errors through clarifying questions rather than direct contradiction
Respond in writing with empathy and resolution, then offer a phone call to discuss details — verbal exchange reduces the adversarial dynamic of written correction

q8.question: Two experienced team members give you contradictory advice on handling a service situation. Both have strong track records. You:
options: Ask each to explain the reasoning behind their recommendation, not just the conclusion
Look at what's different about this situation compared to when each approach worked before
Try the approach with the lower downside risk and evaluate the outcome
Check if there's a documented precedent for this type of situation before deciding

q9.question: A key supplier notifies you that a critical component will be unavailable for three weeks. Your service depends on it daily. Switching suppliers permanently isn't viable due to contracts. You:
options: Source a temporary alternative that covers the gap, even if it's not identical, and adjust your process to accommodate the differences
Redesign the affected service workflow to function without the component for three weeks, even if it means a different (but acceptable) customer experience
Negotiate with the supplier to provide partial or substitute deliveries, prioritizing the specific variants you need most urgently
Build a small reserve by reducing usage in lower-priority applications and redirecting inventory to the most critical service lines

q10.question: Customer feedback shows that your team's explanations during outages are confusing — and you wrote the scripts. You:
options: Record real explanations and review them to identify what is unclear
Practice with mock scenarios using plain-language scripts until it feels natural
Ask a communication trainer to observe one interaction and give targeted advice
Create a template library for common outage explanations and test it with users

q11.question: To reduce errors, you:
options: Introduce checklists
Standardize and 5S
Automate repetitive steps
Trace each error back to the step where it first appears

q12.question: You need to communicate a service-level reduction to long-term clients. The reduction is permanent and non-negotiable. Some clients will be significantly impacted. You must preserve the relationship while being transparent. How do you deliver the message?
options: State the change clearly with effective dates and specific impacts per client segment — ambiguity would be worse than bad news
Frame within a broader service evolution narrative — position the reduction as part of a strategic shift that includes improvements in other areas
Contact the most impacted clients individually first, gauge their concerns, then use those insights to refine the broader announcement
Replace a mass announcement with individual account reviews — each client hears the news in the context of their specific relationship and usage

q13.question: You've relied on an operational procedure for months. A junior team member presents preliminary case data suggesting one of its core assumptions is wrong. You:
options: Pause applying the procedure in affected cases and test both approaches on recent cases before committing either way
Review the team member's raw case data yourself to assess its strength before involving anyone else
Note the challenge formally and continue with the procedure, but define a specific outcome threshold that would trigger a full reassessment
Ask two people who weren't involved in either analysis to independently evaluate the conflicting evidence

q14.question: Management wants to introduce a digital self-service channel to reduce call volume by 30%. But your data shows that 60% of customers who try self-service end up calling anyway because the issues are too complex. You:
options: Redesign the self-service flow to handle only the cases it can actually resolve well, and route complex cases to agents faster rather than trying to contain them
Create a hybrid: let customers start in self-service but offer a seamless escalation that preserves context, so the agent call is shorter
Reframe the 30% target: instead of reducing total calls, focus on reducing repeat calls by using self-service for follow-ups and status checks
Pilot three different self-service approaches on different case types to identify which categories actually benefit from automation before scaling

q15.question: An audit found that your compliance documentation has recurring gaps — a skill you haven't developed. You:
options: Identify the two most common gaps and create a personal checklist for those areas
Ask the auditor to walk you through exactly what good documentation looks like
Compare your documentation with a colleague's who scored well, side by side
Redo one recent document from scratch using the correct standard and time yourself

q16.question: Choosing a new tool, you:
options: Score by requirements fit
Pilot with a small group
Evaluate cost/benefit and risk
Check integration with systems

q17.question: You've been asked to train a new team on a complex workflow. Half the group has operational experience in a related field; the other half are complete beginners. You have one training session. How do you structure it?
options: Teach the full workflow end-to-end at beginner level, then offer an advanced supplement session for experienced members who want deeper context
Start with the conceptual model — why the workflow exists — then let experienced members connect it to what they know while beginners build from scratch
Begin with a diagnostic exercise that reveals what each person already knows, then adjust depth dynamically based on actual gaps
Pair experienced and beginner members, provide a guided practice scenario, and circulate to address individual questions — replace lecture with structured peer learning

q18.question: A new industry guideline contradicts a practice your team has used successfully for years. The guideline's evidence base is limited but growing. You:
options: Review the evidence behind the guideline and compare it with your team's outcome data
Pilot the new guideline on a subset of cases while maintaining the old practice as control
Adopt the guideline but track outcomes closely to verify it performs as claimed
Consult peers at other organizations to understand how they're interpreting and implementing it

q19.question: Your team runs a 24/7 operation. A new regulation requires additional documentation at each handoff between shifts, but adding paperwork would slow transitions and create a 15-minute gap in coverage. You:
options: Redesign the handoff format: replace the paper-based approach with a structured digital log that auto-populates from the shift's activity data
Overlap shifts by 15 minutes and use the overlap period for both handoff and documentation, absorbing the time cost within the schedule
Split the documentation requirement: capture critical items during the handoff and defer detailed notes to a quieter period within the next shift
Redefine the documentation scope: work with compliance to identify the minimum viable documentation that satisfies the regulation without the full burden

q20.question: You've been told your process improvement proposals lack data support — a skill you haven't developed. You:
options: Attach measurable baselines to your next proposal, even if the data collection takes extra time
Partner with an analyst to learn how they build data-backed arguments
Take a short course on operational data analysis and apply it to a real improvement case
Review three approved proposals to reverse-engineer what data made them convincing

q21.question: Evaluating a process change, you:
options: Define success metrics up front
Compare before/after data
Run a controlled trial
Document impact and next steps

**Option B (Recognize behaviors):** You acknowledge and reward helpful actions.

**Option C (Rotate fairly):** You ensure workload is distributed equitably.

**Option D (Quick support):** You remove obstacles that frustrate staff.

**How to answer:** What actually works to maintain morale in your team? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mNeXuCYiE0U

q11.title: Understanding Your Approach
q11.body:
**Option A (Checklists):** You use step-by-step verification to prevent mistakes.

**Option B (Standardize/5S):** You organize the work environment for clarity and efficiency.

**Option C (Automate):** You use technology to eliminate human error on repetitive tasks.

**Option D (Measure/fix causes):** You analyze error patterns and address root causes.

**How to answer:** Which error-reduction method has worked best in your experience? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=M576WGiDBdQ

q12.title: Understanding Your Approach
q12.body:
**Option A (Acceptance criteria):** You define clear standards for what constitutes complete work.

**Option B (Handoff templates):** You use consistent formats for transferring work.

**Option C (Alignment huddles):** You meet briefly to ensure everyone is on the same page.

**Option D (Shared dashboards):** You make status visible to all parties.

**How to answer:** How do you ensure smooth handoffs between teams or shifts? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eIho2S0ZahI

q13.title: Understanding Your Approach
q13.body:
**Option A (Short retros):** You run regular reflection sessions to capture learning.

**Option B (Simple summaries):** You document key takeaways in accessible formats.

**Option C (Searchable FAQs):** You build knowledge bases that are easy to navigate.

**Option D (Assign owners):** You ensure someone is responsible for keeping content current.

**How to answer:** How does your team actually capture and preserve lessons learned? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V55rCBT1Ouc

q14.title: Understanding Your Approach
q14.body:
**Option A (Smarter sequencing):** You reorder work to flow more efficiently.

**Option B (Re-use resources):** You find creative ways to get more from what you have.

**Option C (Rearrange flow):** You optimize physical or digital workspace layout.

**Option D (Simplify steps):** You reduce complexity for users and staff.

**How to answer:** Facing constraints, which approach is most natural for you? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G8w0rJhztJ4

q15.title: Understanding Your Approach
q15.body:
**Option A (Blameless review):** You analyze what happened without assigning fault.

**Option B (Notify transparently):** You communicate with stakeholders honestly and promptly.

**Option C (Recovery plan fast):** You move quickly to resolve the situation.

**Option D (Track to closure):** You follow through until the issue is fully resolved.

**How to answer:** After a service failure, what is your priority? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hiiEeMN7vbQ

q16.title: Understanding Your Approach
q16.body:
**Option A (Score by requirements):** You evaluate tools against a clear list of needs.

**Option B (Pilot small group):** You test with a subset before broader rollout.

**Option C (Cost/benefit/risk):** You analyze financial and operational implications.

**Option D (Check integration):** You ensure the tool works with existing systems.

**How to answer:** Selecting new tools, which criterion matters most to you? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iVXW1r_ivjw

q17.title: Understanding Your Approach
q17.body:
**Option A (Clear dashboards):** You use visual displays to keep everyone informed.

**Option B (Quick standups):** You hold brief, regular alignment meetings.

**Option C (Bullet updates):** You send concise written communications.

**Option D (Radio/chat protocols):** You use real-time communication channels.

**How to answer:** How do you typically keep shifts or teams aligned? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=b1iGRaeNfn4

q18.title: Understanding Your Approach
q18.body:
**Option A (Official guidance):** You start with authoritative sources and documentation.

**Option B (Expert clarifications):** You consult with people who understand the regulation.

**Option C (Compliance checklist):** You create practical tools for implementation.

**Option D (Train with examples):** You help the team understand through concrete cases.

**How to answer:** Learning a new regulation, which approach works best for you? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Stc0beAxavY

q19.title: Understanding Your Approach
q19.body:
**Option A (Simplify forms/language):** You make information easier to understand for everyone.

**Option B (Alternate formats):** You offer multiple ways to access information or service.

**Option C (Rework space/signage):** You improve physical navigation and clarity.

**Option D (Co-design with users):** You involve those who will use the service in designing it.

**How to answer:** Which approach have you used to make services more accessible? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BzAdXyPYKQo

q20.title: Understanding Your Approach
q20.body:
**Option A (SLAs/audits):** You set standards and check compliance systematically.

**Option B (Leading indicators):** You track early warning signs before problems manifest.

**Option C (Improvement cycles):** You run continuous improvement processes.

**Option D (Protect focus time):** You guard staff time for quality-focused work.

**How to answer:** What sustains quality over the long term in your experience? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=L4N1q4RNi9I

q21.title: Understanding Your Approach
q21.body:
**Option A (Define metrics upfront):** You establish success criteria before making changes.

**Option B (Compare before/after):** You measure the impact of changes with data.

**Option C (Controlled trial):** You test changes in limited conditions first.

**Option D (Document impact):** You record what changed and plan next steps.

**How to answer:** Evaluating a process change, which step do you prioritize? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VQKMoT-6XSg

open1.title: How to Answer Well
open1.body: Strong answers describe a specific process improvement with measurable results. Structure: The problem (bottleneck, error rate, customer complaint), Your solution (what you changed), How you made it stick (training, documentation, metrics), The outcome (quantified improvement). Example: 'I reduced average wait time from 12 to 4 minutes by redesigning the triage flow and creating visual cues for staff. We sustained this by adding wait time to our daily dashboard.' Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0vNUxleEyoo

open2.title: How to Answer Well
open2.body: Name a genuine operational skill you want to develop and describe specific actions. Avoid vague answers like 'get better at operations.' Instead: 'I want to improve my data analysis skills to make better staffing decisions. I enrolled in a Tableau course, I practice building dashboards weekly, and I meet monthly with our analytics team to review my work.' Show active learning. Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tJxFZrMxT3s

examplesByCategory.comp_power: Operations Computational Power questions assess your ability to use data for scheduling, error reduction, and process optimization. Strong performers segment problems systematically, use metrics to drive decisions, and validate changes with controlled experiments.
examplesByCategory.communication: Operations Communication questions evaluate how you handle difficult situations, communicate changes, and coordinate teams. Effective operations communicators explain the 'why' behind changes and create clear channels for questions and feedback.
examplesByCategory.knowledge: Operations Knowledge questions measure how you maintain procedures, onboard staff, and capture lessons learned. Top performers create searchable documentation, run regular training, and collect frontline feedback to keep knowledge current.
examplesByCategory.creativity: Operations Creativity questions assess your ability to solve recurring problems and improve service experiences. Creative operations professionals test small changes, borrow solutions from other contexts, and involve staff in developing improvements.
examplesByCategory.drive: Operations Drive questions measure your resilience under pressure and ability to sustain long-term quality. High-drive operations professionals prioritize ruthlessly, celebrate progress, and follow through systematically after failures.

business.hero_headline:
Find the Right Talent.
Before You See Their CV.
business.hero_subheadline: XIMA brings enterprise psychometrics to SMBs. Validated behavioral profiles, AI-generated role-specific challenges, shortlists filtered by real fit — not credentials.
business.hero_subtitle: Leverage XIMA's advanced assessment platform to identify candidates who truly fit your organization's needs.
business.why_headline:
The Problem Isn't
Finding Candidates. It's Finding the Right Ones.
business.why_subheadline: Enterprise solutions cost €50,000–€500,000 per year. XIMA starts at €199/month. Same behavioral signal quality — without the enterprise contract.
business.feature1_body: The AI system matches candidates based on validated behavioral signals — not CV keywords. See who they are, how they think, and how they work under pressure. Before you open their profile.
business.feature2_body: L1 Core Assessment — identical structure for every candidate, comparable by design. L2 Role-Specific Challenge — AI generates a simulation on your actual job post, custom rubric. L3 Video Session — human review only. Each level reveals something deeper.
business.feature3_body: Every AI decision logged with SHA-256 hash, model version, and latency. Deterministic replay for audit or dispute resolution. GDPR Art. 22 by architecture — not by policy. Zero autonomous decisions: every hiring decision requires human confirmation.
business.feature4_body: HireVue and enterprise competitors require €50,000+ contracts. XIMA starts at €199/month. No long-term contract. No paid onboarding. Behavioral signal-based hiring — finally accessible to the companies where most hiring actually happens.
business.pipeline_subheadline: From role publication to evaluated candidate — all in a traceable pipeline.
business.pipeline_step1_body: Create a position on XIMA. Define the professional field, role, and hiring goal. The system automatically calibrates the assessment to your specific context.
business.pipeline_step2_body: Each candidate completes the Core Assessment and receives their XIMAtar. Behavioral profiles are generated before you see their name. Zero credential bias in the screening phase.
business.pipeline_step3_body: For shortlisted candidates, XIMA generates an L2 challenge specific to your role — AI simulation built on your actual job post. Custom rubric. Signal extracted from how they actually think about your problem.
business.pipeline_step4_body: Dashboard with behavioral scores, candidate comparison, strength and friction signals per pillar. Every AI recommendation is an advisory signal — the final decision is always yours.
business.pricing_subheadline: No long-term contract. No paid onboarding. Start, evaluate, scale.
business.plan1_tagline: For teams starting with data-driven hiring
business.plan2_tagline: For growing teams that want deeper signal
business.trust_headline:
Built to Withstand
the Strictest Audit.
business.trust_1_body: XIMA is classified as a decision-support tool — not a decision-maker. Every AI output requires human confirmation. Not added at the end: designed into the architecture from day one.
business.trust_2_body: No name. No photo. No university. No postcode. The five pillars see behavior — and nothing else. Candidates evaluated on what they do, not who they are on paper.
business.trust_3_body: Every AI call logged with prompt hash, model version, latency, and scoring schema. Deterministic replay for audit or dispute resolution. The past is verifiable.
business.trust_4_body: Supabase EU region. No data transfer outside the EEA except AI inference (Gemini API — covered by Google DPA). Row-Level Security on all 78 database tables.
business.cta_body: Start with a free trial. No credit card required. Your first candidate evaluated within 24 hours.

business.feature_2_desc: Evaluate candidates across 5 key pillars: Drive, Computational Power, Communication, Creativity, and Knowledge.
business.feature_3_desc: Access detailed analytics and reports to make informed hiring decisions.
business.feature_4_desc: Reduce time-to-hire with our efficient candidate evaluation and matching system.

guide.title: How XIMAtar Assessment Works
guide.subtitle: Discover your unique professional archetype through our comprehensive assessment system based on 5 core pillars.

step1.title: CV Upload
step1.description: Upload your CV for AI-powered analysis of your professional experience and skills.

step2.title: Baseline Assessment
step2.description: Select your field and complete the initial evaluation with multiple-choice questions.

step3.title: Deep Assessment
step3.description: Answer 21 questions plus open responses to reveal your unique profile.

step4.title: XIMAtar Assignment
step4.description: Receive your personalized XIMAtar archetype based on your pillar scores.

guide.pillars_description: Each pillar represents a core dimension of professional capability. Your unique combination determines your XIMAtar.

pillars.computational: Analytical thinking, problem-solving, and data-driven decision making.
pillars.communication: Clarity of expression, active listening, and collaborative dialogue.
pillars.knowledge: Domain expertise, continuous learning, and wisdom application.
pillars.creativity: Innovation, lateral thinking, and novel solution generation.

guide.scoring_description: Each pillar is scored from 0-10 based on your assessment responses. Here's an example profile:
guide.result_explanation: With highest Creativity (9.1) and strong Computational Power (8.2), this profile matches the Fox archetype - a Strategic Opportunist who excels at innovative problem-solving.
guide.ximatars_description: Each XIMAtar represents a unique combination of strengths and work style. Discover which archetype matches your profile.
guide.logic_step1_desc: Your responses to 21 multiple-choice questions and 2 open-ended questions are analyzed to calculate scores for each of the 5 pillars.
guide.logic_step2_desc: Your pillar profile is compared against the ideal vectors for each of the 12 XIMatars using distance algorithms and weighted matching.
guide.logic_step3_desc: A 25% matching bonus is applied when your strongest pillar aligns with a XIMAtar's primary characteristic, ensuring authentic matches.
guide.logic_step4_desc: The XIMAtar with the closest match to your unique profile becomes your archetype, along with personalized insights about your strengths and growth areas.
guide.cta_description: Start your assessment journey now and unlock personalized insights about your professional potential.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: At XIMA S.r.l., we are committed to protecting your privacy and personal data. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, store, and protect your information when you use our platform. By using XIMA, you consent to the practices described in this policy.
privacy.data_collection_content:
We collect the following types of personal data:

• Account information: email address, name, and profile details you provide during registration
• Professional data: CV uploads, assessment responses, and career preferences
• Usage data: how you interact with our platform, including pages visited and features used
• Technical data: device information, IP address, and browser type for security and optimization purposes

All data is collected with your explicit consent and is necessary for providing our services.
privacy.data_usage_content:
Your data is used for the following purposes:

• Creating and maintaining your XIMAtar professional profile
• AI-powered assessment analysis to provide personalized insights
• Matching you with relevant career opportunities and challenges
• Facilitating communication between candidates, businesses, and mentors
• Improving our platform and developing new features
• Complying with legal obligations and preventing fraud

We use automated decision-making (profiling) to generate your XIMAtar archetype. You can object to this processing at any time through your account settings.
privacy.data_security_content:
We implement industry-standard security measures to protect your data:

• Encryption of data in transit (TLS/SSL) and at rest
• Secure cloud infrastructure hosted in EU data centers
• Row-level security ensuring users can only access their own data
• Regular security audits and vulnerability assessments
• Strict access controls for our team members

Despite our best efforts, no system is 100% secure. We encourage you to use strong passwords and keep your account credentials confidential.
privacy.your_rights_content:
Under the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), you have the following rights:

• Right of Access: Request a copy of all personal data we hold about you
• Right to Rectification: Correct any inaccurate or incomplete data
• Right to Erasure: Request deletion of your personal data ('right to be forgotten')
• Right to Restriction: Limit how we process your data in certain circumstances
• Right to Data Portability: Receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format
• Right to Object: Object to automated profiling and direct marketing
• Right to Withdraw Consent: Withdraw consent at any time without affecting prior processing

To exercise any of these rights, contact our Data Protection Officer at privacy@xima.app.
privacy.contact_content:
For any privacy-related inquiries or to exercise your rights, please contact:

XIMA S.r.l.
Via Torino, 2
20123 Milano (MI), Italia

Data Protection Officer: privacy@xima.app
General inquiries: info@xima.app

You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the Italian Data Protection Authority (Garante per la protezione dei dati personali) if you believe your rights have been violated.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: Welcome to XIMA. These Terms of Service govern your access to and use of the XIMA platform operated by XIMA S.r.l. By registering for an account or using our services, you agree to be bound by these terms. Please read them carefully.
terms.acceptance_content:
By creating an account or accessing XIMA, you confirm that:

• You are at least 18 years old or have parental consent
• You have read and understood these Terms of Service
• You agree to be bound by these terms and our Privacy Policy
• The information you provide is accurate and complete

If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use our platform.
terms.services_content:
XIMA provides a professional assessment and matching platform that includes:

• XIMAtar Assessment: AI-powered evaluation of your professional profile across five pillars (Computational Power, Communication, Knowledge, Creativity, Drive)
• Career Matching: Connection with businesses seeking candidates that match your profile
• Challenge System: Structured challenges to demonstrate your capabilities to potential employers
• Mentor Network: Access to professional mentors for career guidance
• Feed & Communication: Tools to engage with opportunities and communicate with businesses

We reserve the right to modify, suspend, or discontinue any part of our services with reasonable notice.
terms.user_obligations_content:
When using XIMA, you agree to:

• Provide accurate and truthful information in your profile and assessments
• Keep your account credentials secure and not share them with others
• Not attempt to manipulate assessment results or submit false information
• Respect other users and maintain professional conduct in all communications
• Not use the platform for any unlawful purpose or to harass others
• Not attempt to reverse-engineer, scrape, or interfere with the platform

Violation of these obligations may result in account suspension or termination.
terms.liability_content:
To the fullest extent permitted by law:

• XIMA provides services 'as is' without warranties of any kind
• We do not guarantee employment outcomes or business results
• We are not responsible for decisions made by third parties based on your profile
• Our liability is limited to direct damages up to the amount you paid us (if any) in the past 12 months
• We are not liable for indirect, consequential, or punitive damages

Nothing in these terms limits our liability for fraud, gross negligence, or matters that cannot be excluded by law.
terms.termination_content:
You may delete your account at any time through your account settings. Upon deletion:

• Your profile and personal data will be permanently removed
• Assessment results and XIMAtar profile will be deleted
• Challenge submissions will be anonymized
• Chat messages will be anonymized

We may suspend or terminate accounts that violate these terms or engage in fraudulent activity. We will provide notice when reasonably possible.
terms.changes_content:
We may update these Terms of Service from time to time to reflect changes in our services or legal requirements. When we make material changes:

• We will notify you via email or in-app notification
• The updated terms will be posted with a new 'Last updated' date
• Continued use of XIMA after changes take effect constitutes acceptance

For questions about these terms, contact us at info@xima.app.

assessmentGuide.hero_headline:
Not a test.
A behavioral measurement system.
assessmentGuide.hero_subheadline: Designed to be fair, comparable, and auditable. Your XIMAtar is not an opinion — it is the output of a method.
assessmentGuide.journey_subheadline: Four stages. Each one reveals something the previous one could not see.
assessmentGuide.step1_body: AI analyzes your professional background to calibrate context. This is not a filter — it is a starting point. Your CV does not determine your XIMAtar. It informs it.
assessmentGuide.step2_body: Select your professional field and complete the initial evaluation. 21 multiple-choice items on a 4-point ordinal scale. Identical structure for every candidate — comparable by design.
assessmentGuide.step3_body: 2 open-ended responses scored by AI on four dimensions: depth, specificity, self-awareness, actionability. This is where the system stops counting answers and starts reading thought.
assessmentGuide.step4_body: Your five-pillar vector is compared to 12 canonical archetype profiles via Euclidean distance. The nearest is assigned. Top-3 matches are stored. This is you — not a label, a compass.
assessmentGuide.pillars_subheadline: Five independent dimensions. None more important than the others in isolation — but their unique combination determines your XIMAtar.
assessmentGuide.pillar1_body: Analytical reasoning, pattern recognition, and systematic thinking. Does not measure math — measures how you structure thought.
assessmentGuide.pillar2_body: Clarity, empathy, persuasion, and active listening. Does not measure extroversion — measures the quality of exchange.
assessmentGuide.pillar3_body: Learning orientation, information synthesis, and depth. Does not measure memory — measures the hunger to understand.
assessmentGuide.pillar4_body: Divergent thinking, novel approaches, and curiosity. Does not measure artistic talent — measures the capacity to see differently.
assessmentGuide.pillar5_body: Goal persistence, initiative, and internal motivation. Does not measure aggression — measures the engine behind the person.
assessmentGuide.example_body: Each pillar is calculated from behavioral responses, situational choices, cognitive patterns, and writing style analysis. Scores range from 0 to 10.
assessmentGuide.example_result_body: With dominant Creativity (9.1) and high Computational Power (8.2), this profile matches the Fox archetype — strategic adaptability and creative problem solving. Not a category. A direction.
assessmentGuide.archetypes_subheadline: Each XIMAtar represents a distinct configuration of dominant strength, friction point, and Drive level. We don't tell you who you are. We show you the dynamic that moves you.
assessmentGuide.archetype_fox_role: Strategic adaptability and creative problem solving
assessmentGuide.archetype_parrot_role: Expressiveness, verbal creativity, and social influence
assessmentGuide.assignment_step1_body: Responses to 21 multiple-choice items and 2 open-ended questions are analyzed to calculate a score for each of the 5 pillars. Items follow a fixed cyclic assignment — each pillar receives exactly 4–5 questions. Final blend: 90% MC + 10% open-answer.
assessmentGuide.assignment_step2_body: Your pillar vector is compared to 12 canonical archetype profiles via Euclidean distance. Not a black-box algorithm — a geometric proximity measure between who you are and who each archetype represents.
assessmentGuide.assignment_step3_body: A 25% matching bonus is applied when your strongest pillar aligns with a XIMAtar's primary characteristic. This ensures the assignment reflects your real strength — not just your average score.
assessmentGuide.assignment_step4_body: The XIMAtar with the closest match to your unique profile becomes your archetype. Top-3 matches are stored. Your full profile includes strengths, friction areas, and a personalized development plan.
assessmentGuide.governance_headline: The Most Auditable Hiring System Ever Built.
assessmentGuide.governance_subheadline: GDPR Article 22 compliant by architecture. Not by policy — by code.
assessmentGuide.governance_1_body: Every AI call logged with prompt hash, model version, latency, and scoring schema version. Every score is traceable. Every decision is reproducible.
assessmentGuide.governance_2_body: AI outputs are advisory signals only. Every hiring decision requires human confirmation. GDPR Art. 22 by design — not added at the end, built into the architecture.
assessmentGuide.governance_3_body: Any historical evaluation can be re-executed for audit or dispute resolution. The past is verifiable.
assessmentGuide.governance_4_body: No name. No photo. No university. No postcode. No age. The five pillars see behavior — and nothing else.
assessmentGuide.governance_pullquote: "We didn't add compliance at the end. We built the audit trail into the architecture before we wrote the first line of product code."
assessmentGuide.cta_body: The assessment is free. The XIMAtar is yours. Take it once — carry it everywhere.

howItWorks.hero_subheadline: Not a single test. A progressive pipeline — each level revealing a deeper layer of the candidate. We don't define people with labels. We reveal the dynamic between what moves them, what lifts them, and what holds them back.
howItWorks.hero_pullquote: "In XIMA, a XIMAtar is not just a reflection of abilities. It is a living archetype — built from the tension between your highest potential, your current limitation, and your internal force of will."
howItWorks.journey_subheadline: Four stages. Each one reveals something the previous one could not see.
howItWorks.step1_body: AI analyzes your professional background to calibrate context. This is not a filter — it is a starting point. Your CV does not determine your XIMAtar. It informs it.
howItWorks.step2_body: 21 psychometric items on a 4-point ordinal scale, distributed across five pillars. Plus 2 open-ended responses scored by AI on depth, specificity, self-awareness, and actionability. Identical for every candidate — comparable by design.
howItWorks.step3_body: Your five-pillar vector is compared to 12 canonical archetype profiles via Euclidean distance. The nearest is assigned. Top-3 matches are stored. This is you — not a label, a compass.
howItWorks.step4_body: Matched with a mentor based on behavioral signal — not generic availability. The right mentor has complementary strengths to yours. Book a session and start working on your next chapter.
howItWorks.pipeline_subheadline: Each level reveals what the previous one could not measure. The pipeline stops when there is enough signal — not when an algorithm decides.
howItWorks.pipeline_l1_body: 21 psychometric items across five pillars. 2 open-ended responses scored by AI on depth, specificity, self-awareness, and actionability. Identical structure for every candidate — consistent, comparable, fair. Output: XIMAtar archetype + full pillar profile.
howItWorks.pipeline_l2_body: AI-generated simulation built specifically on the company's job post and hiring goal. Custom rubric per role. No two L2 challenges are the same. Signal extracted from how a candidate actually thinks about the specific role — not a generic scenario.
howItWorks.pipeline_l3_body: Asynchronous video response to structured prompts. Communication, presence, articulation. No AI scoring here — human review only. Because some things still require a human to see.
howItWorks.pillars_headline:
The Five Pillars of
XIMA Intelligence
howItWorks.pillars_subheadline: Each XIMAtar is assigned based on: your strongest pillar (your edge), your weakest pillar (your friction point), and your Drive level (your inner fuel). Every score is computed from behavioral answers, situational choices, cognitive patterns, and writing style analysis.
howItWorks.pillars_archetype_logic: This creates 12 archetypes, each expressed in three Drive-paths: High Drive — already activating change. Medium Drive — stable but not leveraged. Low Drive — potential still dormant.
howItWorks.pillars_pullquote: "A XIMAtar is not a label — it is a compass. It shows who you are, what shapes your progress, and what will elevate your next chapter."
howItWorks.pillar1_body: The ability to break down complex problems into logical steps, use data and tools to find efficient solutions, and think systematically about challenges.
howItWorks.pillar1_strength: As strength: you are structured, precise, and fast under pressure.
howItWorks.pillar1_friction: As friction: you may struggle with systems, avoid complexity, or lose clarity.
howItWorks.pillar2_body: How effectively you express ideas, listen actively, build meaningful relationships, and collaborate with others to achieve shared goals.
howItWorks.pillar2_strength: As strength: you influence, connect, and inspire.
howItWorks.pillar2_friction: As friction: you may hold back, lose clarity, or struggle to vocalize your thoughts.
howItWorks.pillar3_body: Your depth of expertise in key areas, ability to learn continuously, and skill in applying what you know to real-world situations.
howItWorks.pillar3_strength: As strength: you synthesize deeply, connect insights, and ground decisions.
howItWorks.pillar3_friction: As friction: you may act on assumptions, avoid research, or stay on the surface.
howItWorks.pillar4_body: Your capacity to generate fresh ideas, see problems from new angles, embrace innovative thinking, and discover unconventional solutions.
howItWorks.pillar4_strength: As strength: you see possibilities, adapt fluidly, and innovate.
howItWorks.pillar4_friction: As friction: you may avoid risk, prefer the known, or resist ideation.
howItWorks.pillar5_body: Drive measures how fast you improve the skills you don't yet master. It tracks your growth over time — not your starting point.
howItWorks.pillar5_strength: As strength: you improve rapidly on weak areas — your trajectory is accelerating.
howItWorks.pillar5_friction: As friction: your growth on weak areas is slow — focus on deliberate practice.
howItWorks.identity_subheadline: Each XIMAtar represents a distinct configuration of dominant strength, friction point, and Drive level. We don't tell you who you are. We show you the dynamic that moves you.
howItWorks.identity_portability: Unlike MBTI or Big Five — which reset with every employer — your XIMAtar is yours. You take it once. It travels with you. Every XIMA-connected company sees the same behavioral truth.
howItWorks.cta_headline:
The assessment is free.
The XIMAtar is yours.
howItWorks.cta_body: Take it once. Take it everywhere. The first hiring tool that actually works for the candidate.
